# Data Visualization Tools for Environmental Sustainability: Empowering Change Through Visual Insights

![](https://tpeurope-em.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/09/data-visual-1024x768.jpg)

# Data Visualization Tools for Environmental Sustainability: Empowering Change Through Visual Insights

In an era where environmental concerns are at the forefront of global discussions, data visualization has emerged as a powerful ally in our quest for sustainability. But have you ever wondered how transforming raw data into visually compelling stories can drive real change? Let’s embark on a journey through the landscape of environmental data visualization, where pixels and plots become catalysts for a greener future.

Imagine standing at the edge of a vast ocean of environmental data. Overwhelming, isn’t it? Now, picture that same ocean distilled into a crystal-clear pool, where every ripple and current tells a story. That’s the magic of data visualization in environmental sustainability. It’s not just about pretty pictures; it’s about turning complex information into actionable insights that can shape policies, influence behaviors, and ultimately, help heal our planet.

## What is the role of data visualization in environmental sustainability?

Data visualization serves as a bridge between raw environmental data and meaningful action. It’s the difference between drowning in a sea of numbers and surfing the waves of insight. But how exactly does it accomplish this crucial task?

At its core, data visualization in environmental sustainability is about making the invisible visible. It transforms abstract concepts like carbon emissions or biodiversity loss into tangible, relatable visuals that resonate with both policymakers and the public. Think of it as translating the language of data into the universal language of visual storytelling.

### How does data visualization enhance our understanding of environmental issues?

Data visualization enhances our understanding of environmental issues by tapping into our innate ability to process visual information rapidly. When we see a graph showing the steady rise in global temperatures over the past century, it hits us differently than reading a table of numbers. It’s like the difference between reading about a beautiful sunset and actually witnessing one – the visual experience creates a more profound, lasting impact.

Moreover, visualization allows us to spot patterns, trends, and anomalies that might be invisible in raw data. It’s akin to having a bird’s eye view of a forest instead of standing among the trees. Suddenly, you can see clearings, dense areas, and how the forest connects to nearby water sources – insights that were impossible to grasp from ground level.

#### What are some real-world examples of data visualization’s impact on sustainability efforts?

Let’s dive into some concrete examples that showcase the power of data visualization in driving sustainability efforts:

These examples demonstrate how data visualization can transform abstract environmental concepts into compelling narratives that inspire action. It’s like turning a complex symphony into a catchy tune that everyone can hum – suddenly, the message becomes accessible and memorable to a much wider audience.

## Which data visualization tools are most effective for environmental sustainability projects?

Choosing the right data visualization tool for environmental sustainability projects is like selecting the perfect brush for a masterpiece – it can make all the difference in how your message is conveyed. But with a plethora of options available, how do you navigate this landscape?

The effectiveness of a data visualization tool often depends on the specific needs of your project, the complexity of your data, and your target audience. However, some tools have emerged as frontrunners in the field of environmental sustainability visualization due to their versatility, ease of use, and powerful features.

### How do open-source tools compare to proprietary software in this field?

The debate between open-source and proprietary software in data visualization is akin to the classic “David vs. Goliath” tale, each with its own strengths and weaknesses. Open-source tools, like R and Python libraries (such as ggplot2 and Matplotlib), offer flexibility, community support, and the ability to customize visualizations to your heart’s content. They’re like a well-stocked kitchen where you can create any dish you imagine, provided you have the culinary skills.

On the other hand, proprietary tools like Tableau and Microsoft Power BI provide user-friendly interfaces and robust support, making them accessible to users with varying levels of technical expertise. They’re more like a high-end restaurant where the chef (the software) does most of the work for you, but you might have less control over the final presentation.

| Aspect | Open-Source Tools | Proprietary Software |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Cost | Free | Typically requires a license |
| Customization | High | Limited |
| Ease of Use | Steeper learning curve | More user-friendly |
| Support | Community-driven | Professional support available |

### What features should you look for in a sustainability-focused data visualization tool?

When selecting a data visualization tool for environmental sustainability projects, it’s crucial to look for features that align with the unique challenges of environmental data. Think of it as choosing the right equipment for an expedition – you need tools that can withstand the rigors of the journey and help you navigate complex terrain.

Key features to consider include:

Remember, the best tool is one that not only meets your technical requirements but also resonates with your audience. After all, the most sophisticated visualization is only effective if it can tell a compelling story that inspires action.

## How can we use data visualization to track and communicate climate change impacts?

Tracking and communicating climate change impacts through data visualization is akin to creating a visual narrative of our planet’s health. It’s about turning abstract concepts and complex data into a story that everyone can understand and relate to. But how do we craft this narrative effectively?

The key lies in creating visualizations that not only inform but also engage and motivate. Think of it as the difference between reading a dry report on rising sea levels and seeing an interactive map that shows how your favorite coastal areas might look in 50 years. The latter creates an emotional connection, making the issue personal and urgent.

### What are the best practices for creating compelling climate change visualizations?

Creating compelling climate change visualizations is an art that combines scientific accuracy with storytelling prowess. It’s about finding the sweet spot between data integrity and visual appeal. Here are some best practices to consider:

Remember, the goal is not just to present data, but to create an “aha!” moment that transforms understanding into action. It’s about making climate change feel real and relevant to each viewer.

#### How can we avoid common pitfalls in representing complex climate data?

Representing complex climate data visually can be fraught with challenges. It’s like walking a tightrope between oversimplification and overwhelming detail. How do we maintain balance and ensure our visualizations are both accurate and accessible?

Here are some common pitfalls to avoid:

By avoiding these pitfalls, we can create visualizations that are not only accurate but also impactful. It’s about striking the right balance between scientific rigor and effective communication, ensuring that our message about climate change is heard, understood, and acted upon.

## What role does data visualization play in sustainable resource management?

In the realm of sustainable resource management, data visualization serves as our compass, guiding us through the complex landscape of resource allocation and conservation. But how exactly does it illuminate the path to sustainability?

Imagine trying to manage a vast ecosystem blindfolded. That’s what resource management without data visualization feels like. Now, picture having a dynamic, real-time map of every resource flow, every consumption pattern, and every conservation opportunity at your fingertips. That’s the power of data visualization in sustainable resource management. It transforms abstract data into actionable insights, allowing decision-makers to see the big picture and the minute details simultaneously.

### How can visualizations help optimize energy consumption and renewable energy adoption?

When it comes to energy, visualization tools are like having X-ray vision for our power grids and consumption patterns. They allow us to see beyond the surface and identify inefficiencies, opportunities, and trends that would otherwise remain hidden.

For instance, heat maps of energy consumption can reveal hotspots of high usage, guiding targeted energy efficiency measures. Similarly, visualizations of renewable energy potential—like solar irradiance maps or wind pattern analyses—can help optimize the placement of solar panels or wind turbines. It’s like having a crystal ball that shows us where and when to harness nature’s power most effectively.

Consider the following ways visualizations are revolutionizing energy management:

### What are innovative ways to visualize water usage and conservation efforts?

Water, the lifeblood of our planet, presents unique challenges and opportunities for visualization. How do we make the invisible visible? How do we help people understand the journey of every drop?

Innovative water visualization techniques are turning the tide on how we perceive and manage this precious resource. Imagine a virtual reality experience that allows you to follow a water droplet from a cloud, through a river, into a treatment plant, and finally to your tap. Or picture an interactive map that shows real-time water stress levels across a region, with predictive modeling for future scenarios.

Here are some cutting-edge approaches to water visualization:

These innovative visualization techniques are not just about presenting data; they’re about creating experiences that resonate with people on an emotional level. They transform water from an abstract resource into a tangible, precious commodity that we all have a stake in preserving.

## How can businesses leverage data visualization for sustainability reporting?

In the corporate world, sustainability reporting has evolved from a nice-to-have to a must-have. But how do businesses transform dry sustainability data into compelling narratives that resonate with stakeholders? This is where data visualization becomes the secret weapon in a company’s sustainability arsenal.

Think of sustainability reporting as telling your company’s environmental story. Data visualization is the illustrator that brings this story to life, turning numbers and metrics into vivid, engaging visuals that capture attention and drive home key messages. It’s the difference between handing someone a lengthy report and inviting them on a visual journey through your sustainability efforts.

### What are the key metrics and KPIs to include in sustainability dashboards?

Creating an effective sustainability dashboard is like composing a symphony – each element must harmonize to create a powerful overall impact. But what are the key notes that should be played in this sustainability concerto?

The most impactful sustainability dashboards strike a balance between comprehensiveness and clarity, showcasing a range of environmental, social, and governance (ESG) metrics. Here’s a breakdown of essential KPIs to consider:

| Category | Key Metrics |
| --- | --- |
| Environmental | Carbon footprint, Energy efficiency, Water usage, Waste reduction |
| Social | Employee diversity, Community engagement, Labor practices, Human rights |
| Governance | Board diversity, Ethical practices, Transparency, Risk management |
| Economic | Sustainable product revenue, Investment in green technologies, Cost savings from sustainability initiatives |

Remember, the key is not just to present these metrics, but to visualize them in ways that tell a story and highlight progress over time. For instance, a tree map could effectively show the breakdown of your company’s carbon footprint across different operations, while a line graph could illustrate the trend in renewable energy adoption over the years.

### How can interactive visualizations enhance stakeholder engagement?

Interactive visualizations are the secret sauce that can transform passive observers into active participants in your sustainability journey. But how exactly do they work their magic?

Think of interactive visualizations as a choose-your-own-adventure book for your sustainability data. They invite stakeholders to explore, dig deeper, and discover insights that resonate with their specific interests. This level of engagement creates a personal connection to the data, making the information more memorable and impactful.

Here are some ways interactive visualizations can supercharge stakeholder engagement:

By implementing these interactive elements, you’re not just presenting data – you’re creating an immersive experience that educates, engages, and inspires action. It’s like the difference between watching a documentary and being part of an interactive museum exhibit. Both can be informative, but the latter leaves a lasting impression that can drive real change.

## What emerging technologies are shaping the future of environmental data visualization?

The landscape of environmental data visualization is evolving at a breakneck pace, driven by emerging technologies that are pushing the boundaries of how we interact with and understand complex environmental data. But what are these game-changing technologies, and how are they revolutionizing the field?

Imagine stepping into a virtual forest where you can see the impact of deforestation in real-time, or using your smartphone to project future sea levels onto your local coastline. These aren’t scenes from a sci-fi movie – they’re examples of how emerging technologies are transforming environmental data visualization. Let’s dive into two of the most promising frontiers: AI and machine learning, and augmented reality.

### How will AI and machine learning impact sustainability visualizations?

Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning are like having a tireless, incredibly smart assistant working on your environmental data 24/7. But how exactly are they changing the game in sustainability visualizations?

Think of AI and ML as the brain behind the visuals. They can sift through massive datasets, identify patterns that human analysts might miss, and even predict future trends. This capability is transforming static visualizations into dynamic, predictive tools that can adapt and update in real-time.

Here are some ways AI and ML are revolutionizing sustainability visualizations:

These advancements are not just improving our visualizations – they’re fundamentally changing how we interact with environmental data. It’s like having a personal environmental analyst that can not only show you what’s happening now, but also what might happen in the future and why it matters to you specifically.

### What potential does augmented reality hold for experiencing environmental data?

Augmented Reality (AR) is poised to transform environmental data visualization from a passive viewing experience into an immersive, interactive journey. But how exactly can AR bring environmental data to life in ways we’ve never seen before?

Imagine walking down a city street and being able to see real-time air quality data floating around you, or visiting a beach and visualizing future sea levels rising before your eyes. AR has the potential to make abstract environmental concepts tangible and personal, bridging the gap between data and reality.

Here are some exciting ways AR could revolutionize environmental data visualization:

The potential of AR in environmental data visualization is like giving everyone a pair of magic glasses that reveal the hidden environmental stories all around us. It has the power to make the abstract concrete, the global personal, and the complex understandable.

## How can readers get started with creating their own environmental data visualizations?

So, you’re inspired by the power of environmental data visualization and want to try your hand at creating your own. But where do you start? How do you transform raw environmental data into compelling visual stories?

Creating your own environmental data visualizations is like embarking on an exciting expedition. You need the right tools, a good map (your data), and a clear destination (your visualization goals). Let’s break down this journey into manageable steps:

Remember, creating impactful environmental data visualizations is a skill that improves with practice. Don’t be afraid to experiment, learn from others, and push the boundaries of how you represent environmental data. Your visualizations could be the key to unlocking new insights and inspiring action for a more sustainable future.

As you embark on this journey, keep in mind that the most powerful visualizations often come from a place of genuine curiosity and passion for environmental issues. Let your concerns for the planet guide your data exploration, and you might just create a visualization that changes how people see and interact with their environment.

# How to visualize environmental data for maximum impact

###### Create Beautiful Charts & Infographics [Get started](https://infogram.com/features)

###### 14.01.2025 by Infogram

Climate change, biodiversity loss, and pollution concerns dominate the media more than ever before. Data is an integral part of reporting on these manners and presenting environmental data effectively is key. Here, [data visualizations](https://infogram.com/blog/what-is-data-visualization/) offer a powerful medium to transform complex datasets into compelling narratives that inform and inspire action. From illustrating global climate change indicators to comparing different regions’ coastline deterioration rates, environmental data visualizations can be powerful information sources when communicating complex issues to the public. That’s why in this article, we’ll explore how to leverage data visualizations for showcasing environmental data effectively, including chart types, tips, challenges, and tools like [Infogram](http://infogram.com) for effective visualizations.

![Cropped shot of a male agronomist using digital tablet for analysis of planted trees. Man updating plants growth on digital tablet app in garden center.](https://infogram.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/GettyImages-1443501849-1024x683.jpg)

## Data visualizations for driving environmental action

Data visualizations don’t just inform—they persuade and inspire action. This is especially important when talking about climate change or pollution levels as these manners require immediate recognition and action in order to change the course of developments that impact the world. Ultimately, an effective visualization can turn complex datasets into compelling stories, driving home the urgency of these challenges in ways data alone cannot.

Here’s why data visualizations are essential in shaping perceptions and fostering action:

### Simplifying complexity

Environmental challenges often involve complex data sets, from global temperature trends to biodiversity loss patterns. Effective visualizations should transform these complexities into easily understandable formats, using clear visuals to highlight key insights. By presenting the data in a straightforward way, visualizations help audiences—whether scientists, policymakers, or the general public—quickly understand the issues at hand, making complex concepts accessible to all.

### Building awareness

Many environmental problems, such as microplastic pollution in oceans or trends in endangered species populations, often go under the radar due to their technical nature or lack of visibility. Thoughtful and impactful visualizations can bring these issues to light, bridging the gap between data and emotional resonance. By creating visual narratives, data visualizations can highlight critical problems, creating greater awareness among different audiences.

### Driving policy and action

Data-driven visuals can be useful in shaping policies and driving impactful action. A compelling chart or infographic can cut through noise, providing clear evidence that convinces the public to prioritize and act on environmental issues. Whether it’s showing the economic benefits of renewable energy or highlighting the health impacts of air pollution, visuals translate complex data into persuasive arguments that drive regulations, resource allocation, and systemic change.

### Engaging the public

Last but not least, compelling visual stories have the ability to captivate audiences, gaining visibility on social media platforms and in the press. Eye-catching graphics, such as maps showing rising sea levels or charts depicting wildlife population declines, can spark conversations and raise awareness. By reaching a wide audience through engaging visuals, these tools encourage people to participate in initiatives and adopt sustainable practices.

![only hands two young women working in the office on their laptop devices unfocused background](https://infogram.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/GettyImages-1415673160-1024x683.jpg)

By considering all the aforementioned factors, it’s clear that effective data visualizations play a key role in communication. Now, let’s explore ways to make these visualizations truly impactful.

## Chart types for visualizing environmental data

Have you ever struggled to choose the right chart type for your dataset? Choosing the right visualization becomes even more critical when working with environmental data, as it can impact how your audience responds to the information presented. To make this process easier, consider the following suggestions based on the nature of your data:

### 1. Temporal trends

If your data involves changes over time, use these chart types to clearly highlight patterns and progressions:

When working with geographically distributed data, these visualizations will help you communicate location-based insights more effectively:

### 3. Comparative analysis

### 4. Distributions and patterns

To showcase trends or relationships in your data, try these visualizations:

### 5. Proportions and ratios

To showcase how parts contribute to a whole, these charts are particularly effective:

Try [Infogram’s AI-powered chart suggestion feature.](https://infogram.com/features/personalized-ai-chart-suggestions) This feature streamlines the process by automatically analyzing your data and suggesting the most suitable chart types in just a few clicks. Simply upload your data spreadsheet or copy and paste it, and the AI will recommend the best charts to effectively convey your information. Learn more [here](https://infogram.com/features/personalized-ai-chart-suggestions)!

## Data visualization tips for environmental data

Selecting a suitable chart type for your environmental data is a fundamental step in creating visualizations that resonate with your audience. However, other equally important aspects also play a key role in determining the overall effectiveness of your message and how well it communicates the data’s story.

Let’s explore key tips for visualizing your data!

### 1. Know your audience

Tailor the visualization complexity and aesthetics based on your audience, whether it’s policymakers, academics, or the general public.

![Use the Business Performance Management tool to Identify Market Trends and Demands. Business development manager on a performance review meeting with their team in a modern business office.](https://infogram.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/GettyImages-2152599933-1024x683.jpg)

### 2. Focus on the story

Lead with the insight, not the data. Ensure every visualization serves the story you’re telling—whether it’s rising sea levels or the success of conservation projects.

### 3. Use color wisely

Make sure to use colors that are intuitive and meaningful, such as green for forests or red for danger. Furthermore, make sure you have sufficient contrast for readability.

![young chinese man works with digital tablet in modern office](https://infogram.com/blog/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/GettyImages-1264414996-1024x683.jpg)

### 4. Simplify without oversimplifying

Avoid clutter but ensure critical details aren’t lost. Annotate key takeaways directly on the visualization.

### 5. Interactive features

Allow viewers to explore data interactively, such as drilling down into specific locations or adjusting parameters to see impacts.

The end result is a professional, visually appealing infographic tailored to storytelling, perfect for education, advocacy, or public awareness campaigns.

### 3. Interactive visuals

Infogram goes beyond static visuals with [interactive elements](https://infogram.com/features/interactive-content). You can create clickable maps to showcase deforestation hotspots, animated charts to depict CO2 emission trends, or real-time data visualization that showcase air quality changes. These dynamic features allow your audience to dive deeper into the data, explore patterns on their own, and gain a more comprehensive understanding of environmental issues.

### 4. Custom branding and templates

We know, consistency is key. With Infogram, you can tailor colors, fonts, and logos to align with your brand’s identity and highlight messaging. Whether you’re representing a conservation nonprofit, an academic institution, or a governmental agency, these tools help ensure a professional and cohesive presentation of your data.

### 5. Accessibility and shareability

Infogram allows you to export your visualizations in various formats, including web embeds, print-ready files, and social media-friendly designs. This flexibility is great for sharing environmental insights and making sure you reach the right audience through the right channels. By simplifying the process of sharing your data, Infogram maximizes impact of your message.

By selecting the right chart types, following visualization best practices, and using Infogram, we can translate complex environmental datasets into impactful stories. #### Breadcrumb

# Environmental Modeling and Visualization Laboratory

Environmental modeling allows scientists and researchers to test hypotheses, run experiments and examine potential future scenarios to better support EPA decision makers in our regions, program offices and laboratories.  Through collaboration with researchers, EMVL staff explore, evaluate, and test new methods for scientific and information visualization and develop the best of these methods into working prototypes.  EMVL projects include a model of the human respiratory system and the Coastal General Ecosystem Model that helps researchers better inform EPA policy makers of anticipated impacts of changes in nutrient runoff.

Scientific visualization is an important tool for environmental research: by representing numerical data in a visual format, it allows environmental scientists and analysts to better understand the results of their research and to effectively convey those results.

### EMVL Projects

EMVL’s main goal is to develop projects that support Agency science and research.  Some have websites where users can download data, examine their own data, and perform analysis.

### Custom Module Development

EMVL develops custom modules, drivers, data translators/filters, and other software tools to support Agency science and policy. Custom System Development also includes the rehosting of software to new platforms (from a vector to a massively parallel environment, for example).  This is done to speed up computation and research efforts.

### Digital Media Design and Production

EMVL is the EPA’s facility for visualization and animation.   EMVL staff create animations, 3D environmental visualizations and tutorials describing how to use the EPA software products EMVL has developed.

EMVL staff regularly produce visualization output in a variety of formats. Using a digital editing system, EMVL staff provide EPA with visualizations that are demonstrated to others.

### Partnerships and Consulting

EMVL is a partner in EPA science and research.  We work with partners including research staff at the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) and the Naval Research Laboratory.  We provide consulting services, including troubleshooting, advice on hardware and software selection, problem diagnosis and resolution, and technical guidance.

### Demonstrations

EMVL staff frequently provide demonstrations of EMVL work to EPA scientists, managers, and guests of the EPA. The purpose of these demonstrations varies, from highly technical analyses of modeling results to high-level presentations of decision-making tools.

# 11 Great Environmental Visualizations on the Internet (2019)

With endless troves of biological, ecological, and financial data, there’s so much to be done to tackle problems of keeping the planet safe. However, sometimes this data overload can be difficult for a single person to comprehend it in the abstract. For that reason, researchers and policymakers have relied heavily on data visualizations in order to convey the scale of the potential for positive environmental impact at a glance.

Several visualizations online in the environmental category have emerged as clear winners, standing out from the average bar graph. While it was difficult to narrow down, we believe that these top ten projects represented both unique research perspectives and artistry.

Here’s Keen.io’s pick of some great environmental data vizzes online today:

## 1: [A Night Under the Stars](http://jordan-vincent.com/night-under-the-stars)

For those readers who love to camp, check this out. Vincent takes a bird’s-eye look at park visitor data and seasonal weather data, carefully calculating the most optimal nights to sleep outdoors in each of America’s National Parks. Employing a radar chart, Vincent shows the reader when and where to camp, categorized by type of landscape and lodging.

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/QLCT72gZSGngrCLKQwAyOOnVJhwvFtpIjgYEH_PnAlGbNQ7OtfioDaZ36NCtBy8Ry23rcSCXmu6lc4ZJOI8xIpQNQ39fF0nmGsStdyB8EWh0rtdOlSIFJflKfrx5xh3xQiy2ZedI)

## 2: [How the Thai Rescue Mission Unfolded](https://multimedia.scmp.com/news/world/article/2154457/thai-cave-rescue/index.html)

While covering the breaking news of last summer, at its core, this visualization it details how the monsoon-like conditions and surrounding environment trapped the twelve Thai boys in the underwater cave. For those who had trouble following along in the text with how, exactly, they got *into* the cave and why it was so difficult to get them out, check out this great visualization.

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/OYZv2YRx6hRTgufPwtj2Za05zoA9pqO-Y6az0EP8ZIgWe-D-FajVuV3moW0u9AY1xKQztb-qPkH2CzE3WbotOC-G_JxCssr3yhFSBT3Q30sSogUzQ3AJorZI9wcjrF0zLivI0itn)

## 3: [What Happens to the Plastic We Throw Out](https://www.nationalgeographic.com/magazine/2018/06/the-journey-of-plastic-around-the-globe/?beta=true)

As part of NatGeo’s investigation of the impact of plastic on the environment, they created this viz to demonstrate the harrowing scale of mismanaged plastic waste throughout the world.

![](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/2BOOOFTtGnFrzgmErdgM9NO9__c4C_KPS2neNzjaCyLGZsY5JEOKduyEROaqaj0j5ENyjWuO6KoYuOPDdzuPJJ51xQXzQxH1qjxrNrqIweCNUyzZobpUb4XXvkpn49i4BUPCAcgG)

## 4: [How the US Generates Electricity](https://www.carbonbrief.org/mapped-how-the-us-generates-electricity)

Often described as the largest machine in the world, this viz demonstrates that the US’s electric system is adopting more sustainable sources for electricity generation. Using a bubble map chart and bar graphs, this viz demonstrates the US’s decreasing reliance on coal as it embraces renewables like solar, hydro, and wind power.

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/8DujSJOG90kGN_trJQstjVyqIKNqTpTuuastMGoVja-DalDeg2BF6oq3aTHMQUsG0_6deBGwtJ01vNA2FqGNzwis2K8quZvX79j9SiPbDvwgnO1AsDmTPal8rjHAB4n7yApMC1p3)

## 5: [The Level is in the Details](https://public.tableau.com/profile/mtmixon3894#!/vizhome/GlobalTemps_3/LevelIsInTheDetails)

Designer Michael Mixon visualizes each day from 1900 until 2016 that local weather channels reported a record-breaking high temperature in each US state. Using radial charts, Mixon very clearly demonstrates the increase in record-breaking highs in the last quarter-century, as red dots tend to be concentrated around the fourth quarter of each circle (each circle runs clockwise from 1900 until 2016).

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/6Uiq6E-R7g1voRUqf86Z93J7gsSTE9lZT2WtrNsZrGIwUJjzfEhon7wXiKxLqiAXuNInegvb1zoCb0AZu3p7gUDqoBQh11MEKG5IJNt4apdTxPC9vGtCxo2imFrkrpZ4kWIB3Tvz)

## 6: [Protecting Common Swift Breeding Sites: A Lesson from Amsterdam](https://public.tableau.com/views/TheSwift/Dashboard1?%3Aembed=y&%3Adisplay_count=yes&publish=yes&%3AshowVizHome=no)

This viz looks at the shifting Swift bird species’ nesting habits in Amsterdam over time, and how the UK, also their natural habitat, can learn how to best conserve the species in developing and urbanizing.

![](https://lh6.googleusercontent.com/QZRe9ZJfiJAyIiw6agRe2lZ6MMczWVP0vP-BlpIdyDm0PyGIb4eq4i3t5q_ocdxs81NYbbdwRyNbNbTfS4ozf2Z-Gz4UsKm1L72G2jkjAo4HZOKvspLluvcwHCwJCvlNwNVmLAE6)

## 7: [A Good Life](https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/a-good-life-for-all/)

This visualization of radial and radar charts successfully compares the social and environmental goals that global nations meet (or don’t). The designers demonstrate the trend where developed countries meet social and humanitarian goals while overspending environmental resources while developing countries struggle to meet social goals but are more conservative in their natural resource usage. 

![](https://lh6.googleusercontent.com/jCGUzo1MY5q5BSt2fXDxdKSzy_W5dUqbQC22td-3Zit4uwPCFi-RCMxpMD48RRz2CLCRPbW1JJBuUP9mzmUza0pPglxXYgQLJ0JiJAXSf17SdVhWrdRXjjtGr548pqhJlm0MkVzw)

## 8*:* [City Eyes](https://public.tableau.com/en-us/gallery/individual-heat-pattern-cities?tab=viz-of-the-day&type=viz-of-the-day)

This visualization visualizes every single daily average temperature in several cities from 1970 until May 31, 2019, in one single, colorful ring. The viz begins at the center in January ‘70 and finishes at the edges in this year, demonstrating temperature rise as the outer circles seem, to the eye, redder. The visualization tracks temperatures in Tel Aviv, Berlin, New York, San Francisco, Stockholm, Sydney, and Shanghai.

![](https://lh5.googleusercontent.com/cAZNzsgFC13fSrNtF5aJvu6oAYSZ0b4kqJsC35yiwlHNUFJ2w5V71nWfYoxSXUaYnb5baeuqoKNqDoyTkSXtj9yTd3hwTU8buz0kc64UUKfurvNhpZRhkskvti2wG6_sHWweppyV)

## 9: [Satellites: 60 Years in Orbit](https://dc.ria.ru/dc/kay-n/2017/satellites/?language=en)

Slightly outside of our immediate atmosphere, Russian data scientist Segodnya made an interactive visualization of the history of satellites in Earth’s orbit, who owns them, and their purposes for being up there still since the first launch of the USSR’s Sputnik in 1957.

![](https://lh6.googleusercontent.com/7unKMhX5HsbC2rGrDvkal0QosWVp258V1ql-qjYDBs9pxdDxOpQ-uFvdAxzYi6FBIm2g2naVvd5RktW9G_lSS2ZG1y4729Qt19PX-Y7v3DgrUxW75lfk-XTsu49KP3yH-MczFGmi)

## 10: [Lights On & Lights Out](https://adventuresinmapping.com/2017/04/18/lights-on-lights-out/)

This map visualizes both the increase and decrease in light pollution at night (blue and pink, respectively) across the globe, using light usage as an indicator for population growth, development, and light and electric pollution reduction.

![](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/7yJFgCms5ohnn2hJXDZ_ekGyWglzCceGWm7UhtT4WYCkhbeeFL5Z44LCfmPmjXzwCfKarwGqBV9VI09lSg9Iotzfa_ocCLX5W6pO1yoYTcweHciizMv1VQ4J74fZHVSMYjUiPcTk)

## 11: [Fighting Open Air Quality](https://openaq.org/#/compare/Estaci%C3%B3n%20Te%C3%B3dula/US%20Diplomatic%20Post%3A%20Colombo/US%20Diplomatic%20Post%3A%20Tashkent?parameter=pm25&_k=7qvv9l)

[Keen.io](http://try.keen.io/contact) customer, OpenAQ, uses open-source data to visually map air quality measurements globally. It can also take the user’s location and map how their air quality compares to two other cities in the world at random, providing measurements on everything from carbon dioxide levels to particular matter (PM) levels.

![](https://keen.io/wp-content/uploads/2019/09/Screen-Shot-2019-09-30-at-1.12.06-PM-1024x492.png)

With innovative mapping techniques and radial graphs, the methods the artists used to build environmental vizzes are equally applicable in any industry, whether it’s SaaS, gaming, or cybersecurity. This process of taking complex, arcane information and making something both quickly comprehensible and beautiful is one that can be done seamlessly, particularly with the right software tools to make it happen.

To learn more about building visualizations like these with your data, click here to [schedule a demo with Keen.io.](http://keen.io/demo)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Graphs and charts effectively illustrate complex environmental data, making trends, comparisons, and anomalies visually accessible, thus facilitating informed decision-making and policy formulation.

Infographics distill critical environmental risks into engaging visuals, enhancing public understanding and awareness, and driving conversations around urgent environmental issues like climate change and pollution.

Case study visualizations provide concrete examples of environmental impacts, showcasing real-world applications of data modeling and emphasizing the urgency of addressing specific ecological challenges.
